records.ety: [F. gros, L. grossus, perh. fr. L. crassus thick, dense, fat, E. crass, cf. Skr. grathita tied together, wound up, hardened. Cf. Engross, Grocer, Grogram.]

defs: Great; large; bulky; fat; of huge size; excessively large.
quotes: A gross body of horse under the Duke.

defs: Coarse; rough; not fine or delicate.
Not easily aroused or excited; not sensitive in perception or feeling; dull; witless.
quotes: Tell her of things that no gross ear can hear.

defs: Expressing, or originating in, animal or sensual appetites; hence, coarse, vulgar, low, obscene, or impure.
quotes: The terms which are delicate in one age become gross in the next.

defs: Disgusting; repulsive; highly offensive; as, a gross remark.
Thick; dense; not attenuated; as, a gross medium.
Great; palpable; serious; vagrant; shameful; as, a gross mistake; gross injustice; gross negligence.
Whole; entire; total; without deduction; as, the gross sum, or gross amount, the gross weight; -- opposed to net.

records.ety: [F. gros (in sense 1), grosse (in sense 2). See Gross, a.]

defs: The main body; the chief part, bulk, or mass.
quotes: For the gross of the people, they are considered as a mere herd of cattle.

defs: The number of twelve dozen; twelve times twelve; as, a gross of bottles; ten gross of pens.
